channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Harden Linux Services with `systemd-analyze security`: From Score to Enforceable Policy
item.description: Harden Linux Services with systemd-analyze security: From Score to Enforceable Policy If you run long-lived services on Linux, hardening often gets postponed because it feels risky: "What if I...

item.title: From Prototype to Production: Building a Reliable RAG API with FastAPI + ChromaDB
item.description: I recently upgraded my Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) project from a simple demo into a production-grade API. This post shares the architecture, what I implemented, and the practical lessons...

item.title: From Applications to Agents: The New Software Paradigm
item.description: For most of the computing era, software followed a predictable model: Humans initiate actions. Applications respond. You open a tool. You enter data. You trigger commands. The system executes...

item.title: Top Fintech APIs for Startups
item.description: Building a fintech product means making critical infrastructure decisions early. The APIs you choose determine your technical debt, compliance burden, and ability to scale for years to come....

item.title: Stripe has been doing amazing work with their llms.txt, and this guide covers it well. This might be worth adding to your own llms.txt file too!
item.description: Stripe's llms.txt has an instructions section. That's a bigger deal than it sounds. Gertjan De Wilde for Apideck ・ Mar 5 #ai #agents #dx #webdev

item.title: I Calculated NPB Park Factors for 10 Years — Stadium Renovations Revealed
item.description: What I Built Using 8,619 NPB (Japanese professional baseball) game scores from 2016–2025, I calculated Park Factors (PF) for all 12 stadiums and visualized the year-by-year trends. The key focus:...

item.title: MCP Servers Have No Security Standard — Here's Why That's a Problem
item.description: Published: March 2026 | By LuciferForge The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is exploding. Anthropic open-sourced it, and now every AI agent framework is integrating it. MCP servers give LLMs the...

item.title: Best Open-Source LLMs for RAG in 2026: 10 Models Ranked by Retrieval Accuracy
item.description: The best LLM for RAG is two models working together. Your embedding model determines whether you retrieve the right chunks. Your generation model determines whether you turn those chunks into...

item.title: Code Is Dead. AI Ate It.
item.description: There's a quiet revolution happening inside every IDE, every terminal window, and every engineering org on the planet. AI software development isn't just a productivity booster anymore — it's...

item.title: AI Will Not Replace Engineers — But It Will Expose Shallow Thinkers.
item.description: Introduction Over the past few years, artificial intelligence has rapidly entered the daily workflow of software engineers. Tools capable of generating code, explaining complex algorithms,...

item.title: Did Live Nation punish a venue by taking Billie Eilish away?
item.description: John Abbamondi had orders to let the CEO of Ticketmaster down easy. In April 2021, Abbamondi was the CEO of BSE Global, the company that ran Brooklyn arena the Barclays Center. BSE Global's...

item.title: A new video from the White House mixes Call of Duty footage with actual video of Iran strikes
item.description: On Wednesday, the White House posted a video of actual military strikes on Iran in the style usually seen in Call of Duty highlight videos, and started the video with a clip from Call of Duty. The...

item.title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic, but his explanation raises more questions than it answers
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Wednesday that his company's investments in OpenAI and Anthropic will likely be its last — but his explanation may not tell the whole story.

item.title: Seven tech giants signed Trump’s pledge to keep electricity costs from spiking around data centers
item.description: Leaders from Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, Amazon, and xAI met with President Donald Trump today to sign a "rate payer protection pledge." It's one way they're responding to growing...

item.title: Tim Sweeney signed away his right to criticize Google until 2032
item.description: Epic CEO Tim Sweeney might be one of the most outspoken people in the history of the world. He fought two of the world's most valuable and powerful companies almost all the way to the US Supreme...

item.title: Grammarly Is Offering ‘Expert’ AI Reviews From Your Favorite Authors—Dead or Alive
item.description: The tool, offered by the recently-rebranded company Superhuman, gives feedback based on the work of famous dead and living writers—without their permission.

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies,’ report says
item.description: Anthropic gave up its contract with the Pentagon over AI safety disagreements -- then, OpenAI swooped in.

item.title: Here’s how Google describes its fee-reducing Apps Experience and Games Level Up programs
item.description: Today, Google killed its 30 percent app store fee, partially uncoupled Google Play from Google Play Billing after they were declared an illegal monopoly in the US, and much more. From July,...

item.title: Big Tech Signs White House Data Center Pledge With Good Optics and Little Substance
item.description: “Data centers … they need some PR help,”President Donald Trump said at the event.

item.title: What AI Models for War Actually Look Like
item.description: While companies like Anthropic debate limits on military uses of AI, Smack Technologies is training models to plan battlefield operations.

item.title: Epic and Google have signed a special deal for a new class of ‘metaverse’ apps
item.description: Epic Games and Google are burying the hatchet, but documents released today reveal that they aren't only aligned on how Google is shaking things up for app stores. The two companies have also...

item.title: Apple Music to add Transparency Tags to distinguish AI music, says report
item.description: The label or distributor has to opt in to tagging their music as AI, so it's unclear how effective this intervention will be.

item.title: NotebookLM can now summarize research in ‘cinematic’ video overviews
item.description: Google's NotebookLM can now turn users' research and notes into fully animated "cinematic" videos, going a step further than the original video overview feature Google introduced last year....

item.title: The new MacBook Air debuts with a $50 gift card as the M4 model hits its best price
item.description: Powered by the new M5 chip, Apple’s latest MacBook Airs are more powerful than ever with double the base storage (512GB), but they also cost $100 more than their predecessor. Fortunately, though,...

item.title: The world’s biggest automaker has one of the dirtiest supply chains: report
item.description: Tesla, Ford, and Volvo occupy the top three spots in a new ranking of 18 global automakers based on their efforts to eliminate carbon emissions, environmental harms, and human rights violations...

item.title: Console exclusives might be making a comeback
item.description: The near future of game consoles could look a lot like the past. Once a hallmark of the industry, over the last few years console-exclusive games have steadily become rare, as the likes of Sony...

item.title: Google Search rolls out Gemini’s Canvas in AI Mode to all US users
item.description: Canvas in AI Mode is available to U.S. users in English for creating plans, projects, apps, and more.

item.title: Decagon completes first tender offer at $4.5B valuation
item.description: The AI-powered customer support startup is the latest example of a fast-growing, young company that's providing employee liquidity.

item.title: 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks Hit 110 Organizations in 16 Countries After Middle East Conflict
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a surge in retaliatory hacktivist activity following the U.S.-Israel coordinated military campaign against Iran, codenamed Epic Fury and Roaring Lion. "The...

item.title: The US military is still using Claude — but defense-tech clients are fleeing
item.description: As the U.S. continues its aerial attack on Iran, Anthropic models are being used for many targeting decisions.
